name: cmu-graphics

metadata.name: cmu-graphics
metadata.summary: The graphics framework used by CMU CS Academy, geared toward beginner CS students.

documentation:
![Desktop CMU Graphics logo](https://s3.amazonaws.com/cmu-cs-academy.lib.prod/desktop-cmu-graphics/docs-media/pkg-logo.png)

Desktop CMU Graphics is an offline version of CMU Graphics, a
persistent-object graphics package geared towards beginner computer science
students. CMU Graphics and its desktop version are maintained by
[CMU CS Academy](https://academy.cs.cmu.edu/), a Carnegie Mellon University
project which develops free-to-use middle and high school computer science
curriculum.

Desktop CMU Graphics works with **Python 3.6-3.11** and is compatible with
[Replit](https://replit.com/) and [Coding Rooms](https://www.codingrooms.com/)
(see the "Replit" and "Coding Rooms" sections below). This
package, including its documentation, is licensed under the
[BSD 3-Clause license](https://github.com/cmu-cs-academy/desktop-cmu-graphics/blob/master/LICENSE).


## Installation

### Choose zip or pip

There are two different ways to install Desktop CMU Graphics on a device:

1. (Mac and Windows only) Use the [zip file installer](https://academy.cs.cmu.edu/desktop) that is available
for download on the CMU CS Academy website.
1. (Mac, Windows, and Linux) Use pip, Python’s built-in package-managing software.

Both methods come with their own advantages and limitations. If you're in doubt
about which to choose, the zip file installer is the most likely to succeed. It
should work regardless of most restrictions in place on school-distributed devices.

For those using devices with Linux operating systems, or for those who are
familiar with the command line/terminal, the pip version of the
package offers a larger degree of versatility.

The remainder of these installation instructions are only for the pip version.

### Install dependencies

If you're using Windows, you don't need to install any dependencies. Skip ahead to "Install CMU Graphics" below.

If you're using a Mac, install [Homebrew](https://brew.sh/).

If you're using a Mac or Linux, install the software packages needed by pycairo. Read their [getting started page](https://pycairo.readthedocs.io/en/latest/getting_started.html) for instructions.

### Install CMU Graphics

Run the following command:

```
pip install cmu-graphics
```

## Getting Started

To run our graphics framework, include the following line at the top of your
Python file:

```
from cmu_graphics import *
```

At the end of your Python file, add this line:

```
cmu_graphics.run()
```

From there, the syntax for using the graphics package is identical to the
online version of the framework. You can find more details about how to use the
graphics framework here on our [documentation page](https://academy.cs.cmu.edu/docs).


## Teacher Support and Documentation

If you are a teacher with a CMU CS Academy Account and you have questions about
CMU Graphics, remember that you can reach out to us through the Support tab on
the left side of the CMU CS Academy website:

![Highlighted teacher support tab](https://s3.amazonaws.com/cmu-cs-academy.lib.prod/desktop-cmu-graphics/docs-media/support-tab.png)

If you are an educator but do not have an account with CMU CS Academy, you can
[register for an account here](https://academy.cs.cmu.edu/register).

If you are a student, or you are exploring Desktop CMU Graphics,
there are plenty of resources to help you get started with
the framework. Students can reach out to their teachers for questions about
CMU Graphics, and a full reference documentation for the graphics
framework is available on our [documentation page](https://academy.cs.cmu.edu/docs).


## Replit

Desktop CMU Graphics is compatible with [Replit](https://replit.com/). To use
the package on the Replit website:

1. Create a new repl. Choose "Python-CMUGraphics" as the language.
1. Run your code!

## Coding Rooms

Desktop CMU Graphics is compatible with [Coding Rooms](https://www.codingrooms.com/). To use the package on the
Coding Rooms website:

1. Visit [this link](https://www.codingrooms.com/compiler/python3) to create a workspace
1. Click "Start Coding Python for Free Now"
1. Click the blue "View Desktop" button above the shell
1. Run your code!

modules.name: cmu_graphics.libs.webrequest
documentation: ## PYPI VERSION ###

functions.name: cmu_graphics.samples.pointillism.centerCrop
documentation: This function takes in a frame (a PIL image) and crops it so the center
of the cropped 400 by 400 image is the center of the orginal image

functions.name: cmu_graphics.samples.pointillism.getCameraFrame
documentation: This function uses OpenCV to grab a frame from your computer's camera
and crops it to canvas size.

functions.name: cmu_graphics.samples.pointillism.drawBlock
documentation: Given the row and column of one of our circle, calculate the average
color of the image in that area, then draw a circle with that color

functions.name: cmu_graphics.samples.pointillism.frameToPoints
documentation: This function takes in a frame (a PIL image) and a "block size" describing
the size of the circles to draw. If the blockSize was 200, we would split
the screen into four 200x200 blocks, and draw four circles

functions.name: cmu_graphics.samples.pointillism.drawText
documentation: A helper function for drawing text centered on the screen

functions.name: cmu_graphics.samples.pointillism.startCamera
documentation: Turns on the user's webcam, begins capturing video, and sets how many frames
to process per second

functions.name: cmu_graphics.samples.pointillism.onStep
documentation: While we're running, clear the current drawing, get a frame from the camera,
and draw that frame as a set of circles

functions.name: cmu_graphics.samples.pointillism.loadModules
documentation: This function loads the third party libraries OpenCV and Pillow, and adds
them to the global namespace so we can use them in other functions.

functions.name: cmu_graphics.samples.pointillism.start
documentation: Try to load the necessary third party libraries and start the application
If loading the libraries fails, print a message about installation

functions.name: cmu_graphics.samples.weather.getAPIData
documentation: Given a name of a city, this function returns information about the weather
in that city, and information about the city itself

functions.name: cmu_graphics.samples.weather.drawWeather
documentation: Given a city, queries the metaweather API to find its weather information,
then draws that weather to the canvas

functions.name: cmu_graphics.samples.weather.onKeyPress
documentation: Search for a city when we press the s key, and try to draw its weather
If we can't find the city, print an error message

functions.name: cmu_graphics.samples.weather.drawText
documentation: A helper function for drawing text centered on the screen

functions.name: cmu_graphics.samples.weather.loadModules
documentation: This function loads the third party library requests, and adds
it to the global namespace so we can use it in other functions.

functions.name: cmu_graphics.samples.weather.start
documentation: Try to load the necessary third party libraries, create our back button, and
start the application. If loading the libraries fails, print a message about
installation

functions.name: cmu_graphics.samples.highscore.startGame
documentation: Set up or reset the game
Here we clear labels from the screen, create our game objects,
set their properties, and set our mode to playing
(rather than start screen or game over)

functions.name: cmu_graphics.samples.highscore.drawText
documentation: A helper function for drawing text centered on the screen

functions.name: cmu_graphics.samples.highscore.gameOver
documentation: Handle a player loss

functions.name: cmu_graphics.cmu_graphics.check_for_exit_without_run
documentation: Checks to see if a user created shapes but did not call
cmu_graphics.run()

methods.name: cmu_graphics.cmu_graphics.Sound.setVolume
documentation:
Volume in the range of 0.0 to 1.0 (inclusive)

If value < 0.0, the volume will not be changed

If value > 1.0, the volume will be set to 1.0

methods.name: cmu_graphics.cmu_graphics.Sound.getVolume
documentation: Returns the volume (range: 0.0 - 1.0 (inclusive))

classes.name: cmu_graphics.cmu_graphics.App
documentation: Based on Lukas Peraza's pygame framework
https://github.com/LBPeraza/Pygame-Asteroids

methods.name: cmu_graphics.cmu_graphics.CSAcademyConsole.showsyntaxerror
documentation: Override the default error handling functions to avoid using our own
excepthook function

methods.name: cmu_graphics.cmu_graphics.CSAcademyConsole.interact
documentation: Override interact so we can exit on EOF

variables.name: cmu_graphics.shape_logic.activeDrawing
documentation:
Shape
├ Group
├ Label
└ Polygon
   ├ Rect
   ├ Line
   ├ PolygonInCircle
   │ ├ RegularPolygon
   │ └ Star
   └ PolygonWithTransform
     ├ CMUImage
     └ Oval
       ├ Arc
       └ Circle

classes.name: cmu_graphics.shape_logic._ShapeMetaclass
documentation: This metaclass prevents 'cmu_graphics.' from being included in the name
of the type, when type() is called on a Shape/rgb/gradient instance

methods.name: cmu_graphics.shape_logic.Group.getPTA
documentation: pass-through attrs (PTA's)
